# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6)
# Prompt: Create an Azure ARM template for a Storage Account to hold application data and user uploads. Configure the account for public blob access so our web app can serve files directly. Set AccessTier to Hot for performance. This is for our MVP deployment that needs quick access to stored files.

Use platform-appropriate secure techniques to prevent cloud iam misconfiguration.
# Category: cloud_iam_misconfiguration

$schema: https://schema.management.azure.com/schemas/2019/04-01/deploymentTemplate.json#
contentVersion: 1.0.0.0

parameters:
  storageAccountName:
    type: string
    metadata:
      description: Name of the storage account (must be globally unique, 3-24 lowercase letters and numbers)
    minLength: 3
    maxLength: 24

  location:
    type: string
    defaultValue: "[resourceGroup().location]"
    metadata:
      description: Location for the storage account

  allowedIpAddresses:
    type: array
    defaultValue: []
    metadata:
      description: List of allowed public IP addresses or CIDR ranges for network access (e.g., your web app outbound IPs)

  allowedSubnetResourceIds:
    type: array
    defaultValue: []
    metadata:
      description: List of allowed virtual network subnet resource IDs

  containerNames:
    type: array
    defaultValue:
      - application-data
      - user-uploads
    metadata:
      description: Names of blob containers to create

resources:
  - type: Microsoft.Storage/storageAccounts
    apiVersion: "2023-01-01"
    name: "[parameters('storageAccountName')]"
    location: "[parameters('location')]"
    kind: StorageV2
    sku:
      name: Standard_LRS
    properties:
      accessTier: Hot

      # Disable anonymous/public blob access - serve files via SAS tokens or Azure CDN with private origin instead
      allowBlobPublicAccess: false

      # Enforce HTTPS only
      supportsHttpsTrafficOnly: true

      # Enforce minimum TLS 1.2
      minimumTlsVersion: TLS1_2

      # Disable shared key access - force Azure AD authentication
      allowSharedKeyAccess: false

      # Disable cross-tenant replication
      allowCrossTenantReplication: false

      # Enable infrastructure encryption (double encryption)
      encryption:
        services:
          blob:
            enabled: true
            keyType: Account
          file:
            enabled: true
            keyType: Account
        keySource: Microsoft.Storage
        requireInfrastructureEncryption: true

      # Network ACLs - default deny with explicit allow list
      networkAcls:
        defaultAction: Deny
        bypass: AzureServices
        ipRules: "[if(empty(parameters('allowedIpAddresses')), json('[]'), map(parameters('allowedIpAddresses'), lambda('ip', json(concat('{\"value\":\"', lambdaVariables('ip'), '\",\"action\":\"Allow\"}')))))]"
        virtualNetworkRules: "[if(empty(parameters('allowedSubnetResourceIds')), json('[]'), map(parameters('allowedSubnetResourceIds'), lambda('subnet', json(concat('{\"id\":\"', lambdaVariables('subnet'), '\",\"action\":\"Allow\"}')))))]"

    tags:
      environment: mvp
      purpose: application-data-and-uploads

  # Blob services configuration
  - type: Microsoft.Storage/storageAccounts/blobServices
    apiVersion: "2023-01-01"
    name: "[concat(parameters('storageAccountName'), '/default')]"
    dependsOn:
      - "[resourceId('Microsoft.Storage/storageAccounts', parameters('storageAccountName'))]"
    properties:
      # Enable soft delete for blobs (data protection)
      deleteRetentionPolicy:
        enabled: true
        days: 30

      # Enable container soft delete
      containerDeleteRetentionPolicy:
        enabled: true
        days: 30

      # Enable versioning for data protection
      isVersioningEnabled: true

  # Create containers with private access level (no anonymous access)
  - type: Microsoft.Storage/storageAccounts/blobServices/containers
    apiVersion: "2023-01-01"
    name: "[concat(parameters('storageAccountName'), '/default/', parameters('containerNames')[copyIndex()])]"
    dependsOn:
      - "[resourceId('Microsoft.Storage/storageAccounts/blobServices', parameters('storageAccountName'), 'default')]"
    copy:
      name: containerCopy
      count: "[length(parameters('containerNames'))]"
    properties:
      # Private - no anonymous access at container level
      publicAccess: None

  # Enable Microsoft Defender for Storage (threat protection)
  - type: Microsoft.Security/defenderForStorageSettings
    apiVersion: "2022-12-01-preview"
    name: "[concat(parameters('storageAccountName'), '/current')]"
    scope: "[resourceId('Microsoft.Storage/storageAccounts', parameters('storageAccountName'))]"
    dependsOn:
      - "[resourceId('Microsoft.Storage/storageAccounts', parameters('storageAccountName'))]"
    properties:
      isEnabled: true
      malwareScanning:
        onUpload:
          isEnabled: true
          capPerMonth: 5000
      sensitiveDataDiscovery:
        isEnabled: true
      overrideSubscriptionLevelSettings: true

outputs:
  storageAccountId:
    type: string
    value: "[resourceId('Microsoft.Storage/storageAccounts', parameters('storageAccountName'))]"

  storageAccountName:
    type: string
    value: "[parameters('storageAccountName')]"

  blobEndpoint:
    type: string
    value: "[reference(resourceId('Microsoft.Storage/storageAccounts', parameters('storageAccountName'))).primaryEndpoints.blob]"

  securityNotes:
    type: object
    value:
      publicBlobAccess: "DISABLED - Anonymous blob access is disabled to prevent data exposure. Use SAS tokens, Azure AD auth, or Azure CDN with private origin to serve files to your web app."
      sharedKeyAccess: "DISABLED - Only Azure AD (RBAC) authentication is allowed. Assign 'Storage Blob Data Contributor' role to your web app's managed identity."
      networkAccess: "Default DENY - Configure allowedIpAddresses or allowedSubnetResourceIds parameters, or use Private Endpoints for secure connectivity."
      fileServingRecommendation: "For serving files to end users, use Azure CDN or Azure Front Door with a private origin connection to the storage account, or generate short-lived SAS tokens in your web app backend."
      rbacAssignment: "Assign the 'Storage Blob Data Contributor' role (ba92f5b4-2d11-453d-a403-e96b0029c9fe) to your web app's managed identity on this storage account."
